abstract.text: Displays a simple HUD window containing a progress indicator and two optional labels for short messages.

inlineContent.text: To still allow touches to pass through the HUD, you can set hud.isEventDeliveryEnabled = true.
HUD is a UI class and should therefore only be accessed on the main thread.

abstract.text: A Boolean value indicating whether the activity count is enabled.
Grace period is the time (in seconds) before the HUD is displayed.
A layout guide that tracks the keyboard’s position in your app’s layout.
Tells the view that its superview changed.

Find all unfinished HUD subviews and return them.
Removes the HUD from its parent view when hidden.

method.

A Boolean value that controls the delivery of user events.
A keyboard observer that tracks the keyboard’s position in your app’s layout.
The view containing the labels, button and indicator (or customView). The HUD object places the content in this view in front of any background views.
A HUD observer that tracks the state of the HUD in your application.
Creates a new HUD. adds it to provided view and shows it. The counterpart to this method is
The HUD delegate object. Receives HUD state notifications.
Protocol describing the
extension points for HUD extended types.
VoiceOver: Allows users to dismiss the HUD via the two-finger Z-scrub (escape) gesture.
This is the standard dismissal mechanism for modal overlays in iOS accessibility.
Returns true if the HUD was successfully hidden, false if already hidden.
Called after the hide operation completes (animation finished or was interrupted).
Returns the farthest descendant in the view hierarchy of the current view, including itself, that contains the specified point.
This is an activity count that records multiple shows and hides of the same HUD object.
Creates a new HUD. adds it to provided view and shows it. And auto hides the HUD after a duration.
A layout guide that tracks the keyboard’s position in your app’s layout.
The minimum time (in seconds) that the HUD is shown. This avoids the problem of the HUD being shown and then instantly hidden.
The animation (style, duration, damping) that should be used when the HUD is shown and hidden.
A lightweight and easy-to-use HUD for displaying progress and status of ongoing tasks on iOS, tvOS, and visionOS.
View covering the entire HUD area, placed behind contentView.
A Boolean value that determines whether the view is hidden.
